name: Panakam
description: Panakam, also known as Panagam in Tamil, is a traditional South Indian jaggery-based cooling drink synonymous with the religious festival of Sri Rama Navami. Made by dissolving jaggery in water and flavouring it with lemon juice, dry ginger powder, cardamom and a hint of black pepper, this sacred offering is distributed as prasadam in temples across South India. It is a natural body coolant, aids digestion and makes a refreshing summer beverage when served chilled.

steps.instruction: In a bowl, take chopped jaggery and 2 cups water.
steps.tip: Use good quality or organic powdered jaggery for quick dissolving.

steps.instruction: Mix very well and let the jaggery soak for 30 to 40 minutes until completely dissolved.
steps.tip: Hot or warm water helps jaggery dissolve faster.

steps.instruction: If there are impurities in the jaggery syrup, filter the solution using a fine strainer.
steps.tip: Always strain jaggery water to remove impurities.

steps.instruction: Add lemon juice to the dissolved jaggery water.
steps.tip: Avoid crushing lemon seeds — they can give a bitter taste.

steps.instruction: Add dry ginger powder, green cardamom powder, black pepper powder and a pinch of salt.
steps.tip: A pinch of salt gives balance to the drink instead of being flat sweet.

steps.instruction: Add a pinch of edible camphor if offering to deities.
Mix everything very well until completely dissolved.
If offering to deities, add a few holy basil leaves (tulsi) for divine flavour.
For serving family and friends, refrigerate for a few hours or add ice cubes.
Strain before serving if desired. Serve chilled.
steps.tip: Panakam can be stored in the fridge for up to a week.

tips: Use good quality or organic powdered jaggery for quick dissolving.
Hot or warm water helps jaggery dissolve faster.
Always strain jaggery water to remove impurities.
A pinch of salt gives balance to the drink instead of being flat sweet.
Avoid crushing lemon seeds — they can give a bitter taste.
You can swap lemon with 1 tsp tamarind (soaked in hot water for 20 minutes) for a different variation.
Panakam can be stored in the fridge for up to a week.
The colour of panakam depends on the jaggery quality — paagu vellam gives a beautiful golden hue.
